task: I need to send a quick update to my class group and save a few links for later, but I want to do it in the easiest way. Can you help me figure out the simplest steps so I don’t mess it up?

turns.agent_response:
Yep — simplest version:

1) Send the group a short update with only the key info.  
2) Save the links somewhere easy to find later.  
3) Double-check the message and links before you send/close it.

Super short version: **update, save, check.**

If you want, I can also give you:
- a one-sentence group message you can copy
- the easiest way to save the links on your phone or laptop

user_simulator.text:
Pretty much, yeah. But I want the actual steps, like super simple.

Can you give me:
- one short message I can paste to the class
- the easiest place to save the links on my phone
- maybe one quick check so I don’t accidentally send the wrong thing

I don’t need a big explanation. Just the clean version.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — clean version:

**1) Paste this to your class:**
> Quick update: [what changed]. I’ll share more if needed.

**2) Save the links on your phone:**
- **Easiest:** put them in **Notes** or **Google Keep**
- Make one note called **“Links”**
- Paste all the links there

**3) Quick check before sending:**
- Make sure the **group name** is right
- Make sure the **message** is short and correct
- Make sure the **links open** when you tap them

**Super short memory version:**  
**Write it, save it, check it.**

If you want, I can make the message sound a little more casual or more school-appropriate.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. Clean and simple.

I’ll use the Notes thing. And the quick check is enough — group name, message, links open. That’s all I needed.
